preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: SaaS trial Users for an Intent-Based, AI-Ready Design System

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global wayfinding context for the session. Validator archetype at decision stage benefits from a minimal variant that reduces distraction and keeps focus on the trial conversion CTA persistently visible — the highest-converting element on most SaaS pages.

sections.name: Hero — Value Proposition with Trust Signal
sections.rationale: Opens the page by establishing clear intent above the fold. The Validator variant of HeroPrimary always includes a trust_signal, which is essential at the decision stage where the user needs immediate reassurance that this is the right choice. The with-social-proof sub-variant places a LogoStrip directly beneath the CTA cluster, compressing the trust loop for Validators who are ready to act but need one more proof point.

sections.name: Social Proof — Quote-Led Credibility Bar
sections.rationale: Immediately after the hero, Validators at the decision stage need external validation to confirm what the headline promised. The Validator variant leads with a quote from a recognizable authority and supports it with logos below, providing both narrative and visual credibility before the user scrolls deeper into the argument.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Sourced Impact Data
sections.rationale: Validators at the decision stage demand quantified evidence, not claims. StatBlock with source attribution converts abstract product promises into concrete, verifiable outcomes (e.g., '40% faster design-to-dev handoff — Source: Internal Benchmark, Q1 2024'). Unsourced stats actively erode Validator trust, so attribution is non-negotiable here.

sections.name: Capability Proof — Benefit-Oriented Feature Grid
sections.rationale: At the decision stage, Validators are comparing MODE against alternatives. FeatureGrid in Validator mode uses outcome-oriented benefit statements rather than feature labels, giving the user a structured checklist to validate that the system meets their requirements. The 3-col-icon layout provides scannable parity comparison.

sections.name: Testimonial — Named Peer Endorsement at Decision Point
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is the primary trust signal for the Validator archetype. Placed after feature proof and before pricing, it sits at the exact decision moment where a named quote with role, company, and photo converts hesitation into confidence. Anonymous quotes carry no weight for Validators — full attribution is mandatory.

sections.name: Pricing — Transparent Plan Comparison
sections.rationale: Validators at the decision stage need full pricing transparency to finalize their choice. The Validator variant of PricingCard includes plan descriptions and longer feature lists, reducing the perceived risk of choosing the wrong tier. The highlighted variant marks the recommended plan, and the trial CTA reinforces the low-commitment entry point.

sections.name: Terminal CTA — Final Conversion with Trust Reinforcement
sections.rationale: CTABanner in Validator variant closes the page with a clear conversion prompt, a reassuring subhead, and a trust signal such as 'No credit card required' or '14-day free trial.' At the decision stage, Validators who have scrolled this far need one final, frictionless prompt that confirms the safety of taking action. The split layout pairs directional copy with a single CTA for clarity.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close with the with-cta variant to recapture departing Validators who scrolled past the terminal CTA without converting. On a decision-stage trial page, this recovery mechanism is critical — it offers one last low-pressure conversion opportunity alongside minimal legal and navigation links.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Decision-stage Validators need focused wayfinding without distraction.

page.section: Hero — Value Proposition with Trust Signal
page.reasoning: Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility at the opening moment for trial conversion.

slots.headline: The Design System That Knows What Your Team Intends
slots.subhead: MODE adapts to context in real time — so your product teams ship consistent, AI-ready interfaces without rebuilding from scratch.
slots.trust_signal: No credit card required. 14-day full-access trial.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This variant directly addresses the Validator archetype's need for peer endorsement paired with recognizable brand authority.

slots.headline: Trusted by product teams shipping at scale
slots.quote: MODE cut our design-to-dev handoff time in half. It's the first system that actually understands the intent behind a component, not just the component itself.
slots.attribution: Elena Vasquez, Head of Design Systems, Lattice

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default and provides credible impact proof for decision-stage users.

slots.headline: Results teams have measured, not just reported

stats.label: Faster design-to-dev handoff on average
stats.source: Internal benchmark study, Q1 2024, n=112 teams

stats.label: More component reuse within 90 days of adoption
Average time to first production-ready component

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default for structured capability proof at decision stage.

slots.headline: Everything your team needs to ship with confidence
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that consistently make the difference between a design system teams adopt and one they abandon.

features.title: Intent-Aware Components
features.description: Components that infer usage context automatically — reducing misapplication errors by 61% across teams in controlled testing. (Internal data, 2024)

features.title: AI-Ready Token Architecture
features.description: Token structure built for LLM consumption from day one, so AI-generated UI inherits your brand constraints without manual correction.

features.title: Governance Without Gatekeeping
features.description: Role-based contribution rules let any team member propose changes while preventing unauthorized overrides — no Slack thread required.

features.title: Adoption Analytics Built In
features.description: See exactly which components are used, forked, or ignored across every product surface — so you invest in what teams actually need.

features.title: Figma and Code in Lockstep
features.description: Bi-directional sync ensures your Figma library and production codebase never drift apart — eliminating the "it looked different in the mockup" conversation.

features.title: Version-Safe Migration Paths
features.description: Upgrade component versions across your entire product with a single audit trail — no breaking changes shipped silently, no manual diff reviews.

page.section: Testimonial — Named Peer Endorsement at Decision Point
page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity, critical for trial conversion from skeptical evaluators.

slots.quote: We evaluated four design systems before choosing MODE. The intent layer is what made the decision obvious — our engineers stopped second-guessing which component to reach for, and our designers stopped filing correction tickets. We recovered roughly two sprint-weeks per quarter within the first six months.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan guides trial decision-makers toward the optimal conversion path.

slots.description: Full access to MODE's intent engine, analytics, and AI-ready token system. The plan chosen by 74% of teams that convert from trial. (Internal data, 2024)
features: Priority support with < 4-hour first response SLA

page.section: Terminal CTA — Final Conversion with Trust Reinforcement
page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Trust reinforcement is essential for converting paid-to-trial at the decision moment.

slots.headline: See MODE's impact in your own codebase
slots.subhead: Set up takes under 15 minutes. Most teams ship their first intent-aware component the same day.
slots.trust_signal: No credit card required. Cancel anytime. Full Pro access for 14 days.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Trial conversion requires recovery mechanisms for users exiting the decision funnel.
